topic: Part 2b Section 3 — Post-Hoc Retrodiction (rewrite per ISS-1010..1013)
change_summary: Decider rewrote SEC-2b.3 to resolve ISS-1010..1013: 'These four are' → 'These two are', WIN-044 description updated (Self-Contradicted per EXP-149), WIN-020 reclassified as fitted-not-derived, hardcoded counts 43/19/67 migrated to template vars {{CA_REVIEWED}}/{{CA_DOME}}/{{CA_POSTHOC}}/{{TOTAL_WINS}}.
our_argument_summary: Of the reviewed WINs, only WIN-044 and WIN-057 have derives_from_dome=true; both are problematic (WIN-044 self-contradicted by the coordinate collapse in Section 1.8; WIN-057 is a coordinate-system definition rather than a prediction). WIN-020 and WIN-058 look geometric at first glance but are fitted/definitional. The remaining WINs state already-known answers without a derivation path from dome geometry to the predicted value. The dome's own 'PROSPECTIVE' label covers only ~13% of its claims, and even those are threshold-continuation checks on already-stable observables, not tests of dome geometry. The section ties retrodiction to relabeling and illusory monitoring as three reinforcing patterns.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Section makes aggregate claims about the code_analysis tag distribution across data/wins.json. CA_REVIEWED, CA_DOME, CA_POSTHOC, TOTAL_WINS are resolved at build time by build-scripts/section-loader.js and build-scripts/generate-html.js. DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT resolves from data/uncounted-failures.json.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Mostly computed. Verified CA_REVIEWED=70, CA_DOME=2 (WIN-044, WIN-057), CA_POSTHOC=68 against data/wins.json. All consistent with the section's wording after template expansion. Two residual hardcoded numbers remain: '(9 WINs)' for the dome's prospective count and '87%' as the derived retrodiction share.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in the section itself. The section commits to 'revisiting this section if those [E-PRED] predictions succeed' — this is a legitimate commitment, not an escape hatch, provided the commitment is actually honored when August 2026 data arrives.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None detected against the current tag distribution. The per-WIN descriptions (WIN-044 Self-Contradicted, WIN-057 Misleading/definitional, WIN-020 Std Model Explains/fitted, WIN-058 Misleading/definitional) all match the current verdicts and code_analysis tags in data/wins.json.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: N/A — this is a prose section, not a WIN. Tag retained for schema conformance.

holes_found.description: Hardcoded '(9 WINs)' conflicts with {{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}} template used later in the same paragraph. After build-time expansion, data/uncounted-failures.json.dome_claimed_prospective_count is 10, so the section currently renders: 'PROSPECTIVE — Predicted Before Data (9 WINs) … none of the 10 prospective WINs derive their predicted values from dome equations.' A reader sees 9 and 10 referring to the same set in consecutive sentences. A dome defender can point to this to argue the reviewer didn't rebuild after the dome added its tenth prospective WIN, undermining the section's credibility on counting.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the literal '(9 WINs)' with '({{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}} WINs)' so both references use the same template variable. ISS-1013 templated the '67 / 43 / 19' counts but missed this one because it sits inside a quoted phrase.

holes_found.description: Derived statistic '87%' is stale. It was accurate under prospective=9, dome_total=69 (60/69 = 87.0%). With prospective=10 (current), retrodictions = 59/69 = 85.5%. The section explicitly frames the 87% as 'the dome's own accounting' — meaning it is computed from the dome's declared prospective count, which has now shifted. This compounds the hole above: the paragraph says '(9 WINs)' and then derives 87% from that 9, while the templated line above says 10.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a new template var {{DOME_RETRODICTION_PCT}} computed from (dome_total − dome_claimed_prospective_count) / dome_total and render it alongside {{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}}, or (b) drop the precise percentage and say 'the overwhelming majority' / 'roughly 85%'. Option (a) is preferable to preserve the rhetorical punch. The decider-style precedent for adding a template var exists in ISS-1013's plan ('add {{CA_QUAD_FAIL}}/{{CA_TRIPLE_FAIL}} template vars').

holes_found.description: The section describes WIN-044's failure mode two different ways in adjacent paragraphs: first as 'self-contradicted by the dome's own coordinate collapse; the FSF cannot be evaluated without resolving the ray-tracing geometry the coordinate system no longer supports' (first paragraph) and then as 'self-contradicted by the model's own refraction fix' (second paragraph). Section 1.8 documents that the refraction fix IS what causes the coordinate collapse, so the two descriptions are not contradictory — but a careful reader will notice they name different mechanisms, creating the impression of sloppiness or inconsistency. A dome defender could argue we don't have a firm grip on what the self-contradiction actually is.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick one framing and use it in both places, e.g., 'self-contradicted by the coordinate collapse triggered by the dome's refraction fix (Section 1.8).' This ties the two concepts together explicitly and removes the apparent mismatch.

holes_found.description: TLDR factual imprecision. The TLDR reads: 'only a handful derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations — and even those use parameters fitted to reproduce already-known values.' The body, however, lists WIN-044 as a genuine V12-geometry derivation that is self-contradicted — NOT as a fitted value. Only WIN-020 (not among the 'handful' in the rewritten body) is actually fitted, and WIN-057 is definitional rather than fitted. The TLDR's 'and even those use parameters fitted' mischaracterizes the two WINs it is nominally summarizing. Severity is moderate (not major) because the TLDR's high-level conclusion (retrodiction dominates) is still correct, but the specific mechanism stated in the TLDR is wrong about the handful.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the TLDR's second clause to something like: '— and even those fail on examination: one is self-contradicted by the dome's own coordinate collapse, the other is a coordinate-system definition rather than a physical prediction.' This preserves brevity while accurately describing why the handful doesn't save the claim.

holes_found.description: 'The remaining {{CA_REVIEWED}} minus {{CA_DOME}} predictions state the already-known answer' renders literally as 'The remaining 70 minus 2 predictions state the already-known answer.' The arithmetic expression is exposed to the reader. Functionally understandable, but reads as if the build system didn't finish its job.
holes_found.recommendation: Either add a computed template var {{CA_NONDOME}} (= CA_REVIEWED − CA_DOME) and use it here, or rephrase as 'The remaining predictions (all but those {{CA_DOME}}) state …'. The template-var route matches the pattern already used elsewhere in Part 2b.

holes_found.description: The opening clause of the derivation paragraph reads 'Of the {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs reviewed, only {{CA_DOME}} derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations.' Renders as '…only 2 derive…' which is stylistically awkward — most style guides prefer spelling out small integers (<10) at the start of a clause or using 'only two.' Not a factual defect.

stronger_arguments.description: The section could cite the dome's own model-page admission of WIN-044's status: per WIN-053/OPEN-011 precedent (noted in Section 1.1), the dome's model page and wins page are frequently out of sync. A specific check — 'as of [date], the dome's model page still lists the FSF as pending eclipse verification while the wins page lists it as confirmed' — would further concretize the retrodiction pattern.
sources: WIN-053 / OPEN-011 wins-page-vs-model-page discrepancy noted in Section 1.1

stronger_arguments.description: The 'prospective threshold-continuation' argument could be made quantitative by naming an observable-variance check: Schumann resonance central frequency has drifted <1% over decades (cite Füllekrug et al., 1995 or similar), so predicting continuity is an effectively null prediction. Similarly, SAA is declining ~0.05%/yr per IAGA/INTERMAGNET assessments. Our current text calls the prospective WINs non-discriminating but stops short of quantifying the null-prediction floor.
sources: Füllekrug & Constable, 'Global triangulation of intense lightning discharges' (1995) for Schumann stability

stronger_arguments.description: The 'audit status' closing sentence says '{{CA_REVIEWED}} of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs ({{CA_REVIEWED_PCT}}% complete)'. Since CA_REVIEWED currently equals TOTAL_WINS (70/70, 100%), that sentence will render as a trivial identity. It could be strengthened by noting that 100% coverage is achieved, which is itself a selling point of the review's methodology compared to the dome's self-certifying pipeline.

kernel_of_truth.description: Retrodiction from first principles does have genuine scientific value, and the dome model is built by a capable AI that assembles nontrivial mathematics. The FSF formula (WIN-044) is a real algebraic construct from the dome's own geometry, not a mere relabeling — if the dome's coordinate system were internally consistent, WIN-044 would represent an honest attempt at geometric derivation. Similarly, WIN-057's two-zone topology is a real structural choice, and WIN-020 demonstrates that gear-ratio modeling can match lunar nodal regression to high precision. These are not zero-signal claims.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The section correctly notes that Newton's retrodiction of Kepler's orbits was validating precisely because Newton derived the orbital shapes from a single inverse-square law, not asserted consistency. The dome's 'derivations' either assume dome parameters that are themselves undetermined (WIN-044, self-contradicted once the coordinate system is interrogated), reduce to definitions (WIN-057), or fit parameters to the known answer (WIN-020). The dome provides no case where a tight, independent dome axiom produces a number that was previously unknown and then verified — which is the case Newton provides for inverse-square gravity.

text_fingerprint.verdict: N/A — prose section, no verdict field

advocate_mode.steelman: A dome defender reading SEC-2b.3 would concede the derivation-vs-retrodiction distinction but push back on the specific counting. 'You say 2 of 70 WINs derive from dome geometry, but you also told your readers (9 WINs) are prospective in the same paragraph you templated to 10. If you can't keep your own numbers straight, why should we trust your categorical claim that only WIN-044 and WIN-057 qualify as derivations?' The defender would then argue WIN-020's gear ratios ARE derived — the dome chose a specific topology (epicyclic with concentric rings) and a specific commensurability condition (18.6 yr) that picks out a point in parameter space; calling that 'fitting' is question-begging because the same accusation applies to any numerical physical model that matches one observable. The curmudgeon response: the distinction is whether the model's parameters are constrained by MULTIPLE independent observables, not whether one observable is reproduced. Newtonian gravity reproduces Kepler AND satellite precession AND tides AND orbital stability — one inverse-square law, many independent validations. The dome's gear ratios reproduce only the 18.6 yr period and no independently-varying consequence. That distinction is robust; the counting error is not.

cross_win_consistency.consistency_notes: All four WINs are consistent with their current data/wins.json tags and verdicts. WIN-044 Self-Contradicted (per EXP-149) — section text reflects this. WIN-020 Std Model Explains + derives_from_dome=false — section text now correctly describes as fitted. WIN-057 Misleading + derives_from_dome=true — section correctly calls it definitional. WIN-058 Misleading + derives_from_dome=false — section correctly calls it a definitional identity. No cross-WIN contradiction found in the section's per-WIN descriptions.
cross_refs_to_other_sections: Section 1.8 (referenced for WIN-044's coordinate collapse) — verified present
Section 4.2 (referenced for E-PRED re-visit commitment) — should be verified by integrity check

quantitative_verification.claim_CA_REVIEWED_eq_70: Verified: len([w for w in wins if code_analysis.reviewed]) = 70
quantitative_verification.claim_87pct_retrodictions: STALE. Correct value under current prospective=10, dome_total=69 is 59/69 = 85.5%. The 87% value requires prospective=9.
quantitative_verification.arithmetic_exposure: '{{CA_REVIEWED}} minus {{CA_DOME}}' renders as '70 minus 2' — arithmetic expression visible to reader.

tldr_review.tldr_text_observed: Of all reviewed WINs, only a handful derive their predicted values from the dome's own geometric equations — and even those use parameters fitted to reproduce already-known values. The rest are retrodictions: the author looked up the answer, then wrote a 'prediction' matching it. Without a derivation chain from dome geometry to predicted value, a correct number is curve-fitting, not prediction.
tldr_review.issue: The second clause ('and even those use parameters fitted to reproduce already-known values') misstates why the 'handful' (WIN-044, WIN-057) fails. Neither is a parameter-fitted WIN in the body's analysis: WIN-044 is an algebraic V12 derivation that is self-contradicted, and WIN-057 is a coordinate-system definition. Parameter-fitting is the charge against WIN-020 — which is NOT in the handful.
tldr_review.recommendation: See hole #4 above for rewording suggestion.

deeper_analysis: The rewrite successfully resolves the contradictions flagged in ISS-1010..1013 at the per-WIN level — WIN-044 and WIN-057 are now correctly named as the CA_DOME=2 members, WIN-020 is correctly reframed as fitted-not-derived, and the stale counts 43/19/67 are templated. However, the rewrite introduces (or fails to eliminate) a new small-numeric consistency problem inside the same section: '(9 WINs)' is hardcoded while '{{DOME_PROSPECTIVE_COUNT}}' in the following paragraph templates to 10, so the two references disagree by one. The companion '87%' figure is a hand-derived statistic that was accurate when prospective=9 but is now stale. This is precisely the class of drift the project criticizes in the dome model, and a defender looking for a counter-attack on our review will find it here. The fix is mechanical and small. The core scientific argument — derivation vs retrodiction, Newton/Kepler analogy, threshold-continuation non-test — is solid and should be preserved as-is. The TLDR's mischaracterization of the handful as 'fitted' is a more substantive error because TLDRs are read first and often not expanded; recommend rewording. The WIN-044 description inconsistency (coordinate collapse vs refraction fix) is a stylistic smell rather than a factual error — Section 1.8 makes clear these are the same phenomenon — but homogenizing the wording strengthens the section.

citations_verified: WIN-020 verdict = Std Model Explains, derives_from_dome=false (data/wins.json)
Template var wiring in build-scripts/section-loader.js and build-scripts/generate-html.js
citations_failed: Hardcoded '87%' contradicts the 59/69 ≈ 85.5% implied by current data
citations_unchecked: Dome 'PROSPECTIVE — Predicted Before Data' label on john09289.github.io/predictions — not fetched this run; the poller is responsible for updating dome_claimed_prospective_count. If the count on the dome site is still 9, then the hardcoded '(9 WINs)' is right and uncounted-failures.json is stale. Either way the section has an internal disagreement that should be resolved by a single source of truth.
